title: Wild Rift Patch Notes 6.1d
paragraphs: Reminder: content will be released throughout the patch.

Once a member of Icathia’s governing council, Zilean is a prodigious elemental mage who seeks mastery over time itself. After using his powers in an attempt to save his people from the Void, he now drifts through the past, present, and future, bending and warping the flow of time around him. Zilean has traveled from Runeterra’s mysterious creation, all the way to its seemingly inevitable ending, searching tirelessly for any strand of fate that might undo his homeland’s destruction.
Zilean will be released on June 13 at 00:01 UTC
A new challenger is entering the arena! Battlecast Nasus is your new reward in this Wild Pass! Queue up and play games with your friends to earn Battlecast Nasus and an array of other goodies.
The Wild Pass will be released on June 13 at 00:01 UTC
A new companion has joined the fight, Winged Grace Iridescygn! They’ll be in the game on June 13, 00:01 UTC. Pick them up to join you on your star-studded adventures on the Rift.

How is your journey in the stars going? We hope you’ve been taking a moment to stop and take it all in. While there’s a whole galaxy out there, there is always room for more stars. So, this patch we’re adding One For All and URF to the Adventure Star. Queue up with your friends to earn rewards and have fun.

Welcome to Patch 6.1d! In this patch we have our Ambessa rework as well as a mini one for Fiora. Additionally, we’re continuing to keep an eye on the jungle and want to give a few more champions some buffs to help them be more viable picks, like Darius and Jax. Additionally we know that some of our other junglers have been struggling to keep up, so we’re also giving a handful of buffs to Nunu & Willump, Fiddlesticks, Pantheon, and Diana. With Fiddlesticks and Nunu & Willump getting some attention this patch, keep an extra eye out for a scarecrow in the bush or the wild snowball gank. To round things out, we’ve also got a few buffs for some mid laners and dragon laners who needed an extra boost, and we’re nerfing some of the champions that have been doing scary amounts of damage (looking at you, Nocturne; you’re scary enough as is).

In the current patch, Ambessa’s late-game cleanup and passive damage exceed reasonable counterplay, drifting off from her core design of precise positioning to trigger her passive. We're removing her passive refresh mechanic and shifting part of the damage into her first ability’s burst for better fight rhythm, while adding armor penetration and ability vamp to balance the tradeoff, plus her second ability gains new outplay depth.
Drakehound's Step
[Adjustment] Bonus damage: 1 + champion level × 3 + (1.5%+0.02%bonus Attack Damage) target maximum Health → 11.5 + champion level × 2.5 + 25% bonus Attack Damage
Cunning Sweep / Sundering Slam

[Adjustment] Damage in the semi-circle range of Cunning Sweep: 40/65/90/115 + 60%bonus Attack Damage → (40/50/60/70 + 30% bonus Attack Damage) + (1.5/2/2.5/3% + 0.02%bonus Attack Damage) target maximum Health
[Adjustment] Blade edge damage of Cunning Sweep: 70/105/140/175 + 90% bonus Attack Damage → (80/100/120/140 + 60% bonus Attack Damage) + (3/4/5/6%+0.04%bonus Attack Damage) target maximum Health
[Adjustment] Damage along the path of Sundering Slam: 55/80/105/130+60%bonus Attack Damage → (45/60/75/90 + 45% bonus Attack Damage) + (1.5/2/2.5/3%+0.02%bonus Attack Damage) target maximum Health

[Adjustment] Damage to the first target hit by Sundering Slam: 80/120/160/200 + 100% bonus Attack Damage to 95/160/225/290 + 200%bonus Attack Damage → (90/120/150/180+90% bonus Attack Damage) + (3/4/5/6%+0.04% bonus Attack Damage) target maximum Health
[New] Damage ratio cap upon monsters’ maximum Health is now 55~265 (increase with champion level)
[New] Immune to all Crowd Control effect for 0.5s when casting
[Adjustment] Cooldown: 14/13/12/11s → 15/14/13/12s
[Adjustment] Damage of the shockwave: 60/90/120/150 + 50%bonus Attack Damage → 70/100/130/160 + 80% bonus Attack Damage
[Adjustment] Shockwave damage dealt when damage successfully blocked: 95/145/195/245+ 80% bonus Attack Damage  → 105/150/195/240 + 120% bonus Attack Damage
[Removed] Passive: If the enemy champions just received damage from Ambessa are not taken down in 3s, all energy will be restored and reset all basic abilities.
[Removed] Passive: restore Health according to Health lost
[New] Passive: gain 10%/20%/30% Armor Penetration and 10%/12.5%/15% Ability vamp effect, health restore from minions is reduced to 25%, from monsters reduced to 40%
[New] Active: 30%/40%/50% damage reduction gained when Ambessa is unstoppable
Ambessa will be released on June 26 at 00:01 UTC

En Garde! We’ve reworked the Duelist’s weak spot mechanic to allow frequent weak spot hits in late-game teamfights. The passive now marks enemies’ vital points more quickly, and the ultimate removes the activation delay— now Fiora is able to break vital points immediately. The movement speed of Lunge also now scales with move speed, increasing flexibility after multiple vital hits. The range for targeting vitals has been increased, and you can now select neutral temporary targets.
[Adjustment] Reveals and marks Vitals on nearby enemy champions for: 1.75s → 2s
[New] The duration of Vitals mark will reduce with Grand Challenge level up
[New] Speed of Lunge will be impacted by Movement speed
[New] May select a neutral or enemy temporary target, with lowest priority, including wards, traps and fruits etc.
[Adjustment] Search range of Vitals is increased
[Adjustment] Basic damage: 85/95/105/115 → 75/85/95/10
[Removed] Cancel the 0.5s waiting for marking Vitals
[New] Passive: 0.25/0.5/0.75s reduced for marking Vitals
[Fixed] Ult no longer stacks Runes
Attack Damage boosted with full stack of Hemorrhage: 22~226 → 32~236
Damage to monsters: 150% → 180%
Damage to monsters: 125% → 175%
Damage bonus: 5% bonus Health → 6% bonus Health
Bonus health regeneration: 11% bonus Health → 12% bonus Health
Bonus shield value: 30/40/50% bonus Health → 35/45/55% bonus Health
Base damage per second: 50/80/110/140 → 60/90/120/150
Health regeneration of damaging enemy champions: 35%/40%/45%/50% → 40%/45%/50%/55%
Damage: 15 + champion level × 15+ 50% Ability Power → 20 + champion level × 15 + 50% Ability Power
Damage: 40/75/110/145+25% Ability Power → 40/80/120/160+30% Ability Power
Health per level: 128 → 136

Damage: 40 + champion level × 15 +100% bonus Attack Damage + (30 + champion level × 4) % Ability Power → 40 + champion level × 15 + 100% bonus Attack Damage + (30 + champion level × 4.5) % Ability Power
Movement speed gained: 40/50/60/70% → 50/60/70/80%
Base health: 600 → 630
Damage of release the orb: 40/75/110/145 + 40% Ability Power → 40/75/110/145 + 45% Ability Power
Damage of retrieving the orb: 40/75/110/145 + 40% Ability Power → 40/75/110/145 + 45% Ability Power
[New] Target searching range will be temporarily increased when Ahri’s basic attack or Charm hits enemy champions or epic monsters
[Adjustment] Fox-Fire will be cast quicker with enemy targeted
Shield value: 100~485 → 115~500+100% Critical Strike rate
Damage: 25/60/95/130 + 100%Attack Damage → 25/60/95/130 + 105%Attack Damage
Damage of Critical Strike: 25/55/85/115 +175% Attack Damage → 25/55/85/115 +183.75% Attack Damage
Cooldown of casting on the same enemy: 8/7/6/5s → 7/6/5/4s
Bonus armor penetration gained with Critical Strike: 30% → 50%
Movement speed increased per level-up: 3.5% → 4%
Damage bonus: 90/105/120/135% Attack Damage → 100/115/130/145% Attack Damage
Damage bonus: 90/100/110/120%bonus Attack Damage → 100/110/120/130%bonus Attack Damage
Base Attack Damage: 54 → 58
Health per level: 120 → 128
Armor per level: 4.3 → 5
Base damage of each wave: 60/100/140/180 → 70/110/150/190
Health regeneration under Frenzy: 20/30/40/50+7/8/9/10% lost Health → 20/30/40/50+5/6/7/8% lost Healt
Shield duration: 3s → 2.5s
Additional Attack Damage gained on Dusk Trails: 20/35/50/65 → 20/30/40/50
Base Attack Damage: 54 →  50
Damage per second: 25/40/55/70 + 60% bonus Attack Damage → 20/35/50/65+55%bonus Attack Damage
Shredding target’s Armor and Magic Resist: 10/15/20/25 → 10/14/18/22
Shield: 60/90/120/150 + 35% Ability Power → 50/80/110/140 + 25% Ability Power
26 New Runes added. The detailed description of the effect and stats of the runes can be found in Rune collections after the update. A new chain effect was also added to the new runes.
Greatly increases your luck, empowering all random effects.
Collect 3 Unknown Augments to draw an additional Augment.
Collect 4 Unknown Augments to upgrade an additional Augment.
Unknown augment collection progress +1.
Upon taking damage at close range, gain a burst of Movement Speed and a shield.
Takedowns greatly increase Attack Speed and Movement Speed.
Greatly reduces the cooldowns of crowd control abilities.
Deal bonus damage to crowd-controlled enemies. This damage Critically Strikes.
Using an ability restores Health
Greatly reduces this Augment's cooldown, and greatly increases its healing effect.
Moving reduces healing ability cooldowns.
The faster you are, the higher your Heal Power is.
Hitting an enemy with an ability grants Basic Ability Haste.
Hitting an enemy with an ability grants Ultimate Ability Haste.
Taking damage greatly increases your Attack Speed and Movement Speed.
Taking damage greatly increases your Armor, Magic Resist, and Omnivamp.
Greatly reduces incoming damage while shielded.
Gain a shield upon leaving combat. The more bonus Health you have, the more damage the shield absorbs.
Summon a wild creature at the target location.
Summon one additional wild creature for every enemy near the target location.
After casting an ultimate ability, become invulnerable for a period of time.
Greatly extends the duration of this Augment's invulnerability effect.
Your ultimate ability can be cast twice every once in a while.
Your attacks reduce your ultimate ability's cooldown.
When taking damage, gain Ability Haste for your ultimate ability. Upon casting your ultimate ability, gain Armor and Magic Resist.
After using your ultimate ability, your damage can Critically Strike for a short duration.
Gain Omnivamp and the ability to execute enemies when you use your ultimate ability.
Greatly increases your ultimate ability’s damage, heals, shields, and Ability Haste.
The more bonus Health you have, the faster you are and the more Ability Haste you have.
Greatly increases your bonus Health.
The higher your Mana, the higher your Health.
Upon taking lethal damage, the overkill damage is deducted from your Mana instead.
You can purchase all the items from the Tear of the Goddess series.
Gain 2 more item slots.
Gain the active ability, Spinning Starblade. Spinning Starblade: Dash and deal damage to nearby enemies.
Deal damage to enemies nearby during dashes.
Gain the active ability, Froststar Wish. Froststar Wish: Heal nearby allied champions over time.
When healing an ally, deal damage to enemies near them.
Gain the active ability, Star Fission. Star Fission: Burrow underground and knock up nearby enemies after a delay.
Heal yourself when damaging a crowd-controlled enemy.
Gain the active ability, Astral Pulse. Astral Pulse: Target an enemy and deal damage over time.
Emits additional homing pulses at enemies nearby the target.
Gain the active ability, Cosmic Cleave. Cosmic Cleave: Become untargetable and deal damage to nearby enemies.
Takedowns reset Cosmic Cleave's cooldown. Dealing damage with Cosmic Cleave restores Health.
Gain the active ability, Galactic Flux. Galactic Flux: Teleport nearby teammates to a target area.
Cast Exhaust on enemies in the target area and grant teleported teammates a strong shield and immunity to crowd control.
Magnetic Blaster's Energized Attacks generate much faster.
Empowers the lightning damage of all Augments, items, and champion abilities.
Empowered Salvation: Triggering Salvation reduces basic ability cooldowns. Casting basic abilities have a chance to trigger Salvation.
Triggering Salvation reduces the cooldowns of ultimate abilities.
Effects stack much faster.
The longer the game, the more damage you deal.
This augment is now removed.
Armor Penetration: 18
Whenever a champion dies within 3 seconds of you having damaged them, refunds (20%+0.2% Armor Penetration) of your ultimate ability's total cooldown.
Outer turret and inner turret now have additional armor and magic resist, which will take effect in the first 3/6 minutes of the game respectively. Then Gradually decay as the game progresses.
Outer turret: Gain additional armor and magic resist that decays from 300 to 0 over 3 minutes.
Inner turret: Gain additional armor and magic resist that decays from 600 to 0 over 6 minutes.

Blood Moon Zilean
				Releasing June 13 @ 00:01 UTC
